nuggets.headline: From Metalcore Band 'In Alcatraz 1962' to Producing for Anderson .Paak
nuggets.text: Before crafting beats, Pete Rango's musical foundation was in a high school metal/hardcore band called In Alcatraz 1962. That experience was the direct catalyst for his obsession with production, pushing him to learn how to record, mix, and master his own records. This evolution from live band percussion to in-the-box production defines his hands-on, multi-genre approach, which eventually led to him creating demos with artists like Anderson .Paak.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, reflecting his hands-on production approach.

nuggets.headline: The Philosophy Driving The Beat: Disrupting The System with XUE Records
nuggets.text: This track is more than just a beat; it's a product of Rango's core mission to "disrupt, uplift, and make the system nervous." He founded the nonprofit L.I.V. Corp and his own label, XUE Records, to build a world where community and technology collide, completely outside the traditional industry machine. As you listen, hear the arrangement as a manifestation of that DIY ethos — a self-contained world built from the ground up.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' Resonates, You Need Samiyam's 'Animals Have Feelings'
nuggets.text: The off-kilter rhythm and textural grit of this track are direct descendants of the L.A. beat scene. For a deeper cut into that sound, get into Ann Arbor producer Samiyam. His 2016 album for Stones Throw, "Animals Have Feelings," is a masterclass in the kind of warped, video-game-influenced instrumental hip-hop that clearly informs Rango's work on "Savage Planet."
nuggets.imageCaption: Producer Samiyam, a key figure in the experimental beat scene.

nuggets.headline: The Fort Myers Pivot: How 'PayUp Game' Shifted Rango's Focus to Hip-Hop
nuggets.text: After being kicked out of his father's house the summer before college, Rango relocated to Fort Myers, Florida. It was there he discovered the hip-hop collective PayUp Game, a pivotal encounter that shifted his musical focus from college mashups to serious artist development and hip-hop production. This period was the direct precursor to competing in and winning i-Standard's producer competition with his partner Parris Pierce.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango at work, a producer shaped by diverse musical scenes.

nuggets.headline: Listen For The HBO Polish: Rango's 'Vi$ions' Placement on 'Insecure'
nuggets.text: As you absorb the production on this track, know that this is the same ear that landed a placement on HBO's critically acclaimed series *Insecure*. Rango's song 'Vi$ions', a collaboration with the artist Cherele, was featured on the show, a direct result of connections made after winning a producer showcase in Miami. This is the sound of an artist whose work is polished for major syncs, a skill honed through years of freelancing in the audio/visual world.

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' Is Your Tempo, Get Into Mndsgn's 'Body Wash'
nuggets.text: The track's relaxed, synth-led groove finds a direct parallel in the work of producer Mndsgn. His album `Body Wash` is a masterclass in modern boogie-funk and instrumental hip-hop, built on a similar foundation of warm keys and tight, pocketed drums. Mndsgn, like Rango, builds entire worlds with his sound, operating in that same space where beat-making collides with sophisticated composition.
nuggets.imageCaption: Producer Mndsgn, a sonic relative of Pete Rango's beat-driven worlds.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's Live Sound Resume Includes Both Kodak Black and David Duchovny
nuggets.text: Beyond his production work, Pete Rango has spent over four years as a freelance live audio engineer. His resume shows immense versatility, running front-of-house for hip-hop acts like Denzel Curry and XXXTentacion, and also for actor-turned-musician David Duchovny. This hands-on experience in drastically different live environments informs the polish and adaptability in his studio recordings.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, where his diverse live audio experience informs his production.

nuggets.headline: On 'Savage Planet,' Listen for the Xmane Collaboration on 'Planet X' and 'Traag Park'
nuggets.text: While you're in the world of the 2022 album "Savage Planet," listen for how Rango uses collaboration to build out the record's texture. On the tracks "Planet X" and "Traag Park," he brings in artist Xmane, creating a distinct interplay that contrasts with the solo construction of "Oms at Play." This demonstrates his role as a culture-builder, curating specific creative collisions within a single project.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' Connects, Explore Teebs' 2011 LP 'Ardour'
nuggets.text: The layered, textural feel of Rango's production has a clear ancestor in the L.A. beat scene, specifically in the work of Brainfeeder artist Teebs. His album 'Ardour' is a masterclass in 'painted sound,' where beat structures dissolve into warm, melodic washes. It's the same brand of experimentalism that blurs the line between instrumental hip-hop and ambient electronics, a space Rango operates in expertly.
nuggets.imageCaption: Brainfeeder artist Teebs, whose work bridges instrumental hip-hop and ambient electronics.
